title: Access Point Health and Availability
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:devicehealth`. from the `devicehealth` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/device-health`. Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~1 event per managed device per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `overallHealth` (integer 0-100), `reachabilityHealth` (string), `deviceName` (string), `deviceType` (string), `siteId` (string), `platformId` (string), `managementIpAddress` (string), `softwareVersion` (string). Filter `deviceFamily="Unified AP"` for AP-specific health views.
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth" deviceType="Unified AP" OR deviceType="ACCESS_POINT" | stats latest(overallHealth) as health_score latest(reachabilityHealth) as reachability count as poll_count by deviceName, siteId | eval ap_status=case(reachability="Unreachable","Down",health_score<50,"Degraded",1==1,"Healthy") | sort health_score
description: Monitors the health and availability of wireless access points using Catalyst Center device health data, filtered to AP device types.
value: Access points are the wireless user's connection to the network. AP failures immediately affect user connectivity. Proactive health monitoring prevents wireless outages.
implementation: This UC uses the standard `devicehealth` input (already enabled for UC-5.13.1). Access points appear as `deviceType="Unified AP"` or `"ACCESS_POINT"` in the device health data. No additional input is needed. Data path: Catalyst Center Intent API `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/device-health` → TA poller → `index=catalyst` `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:devicehealth`.
visualization: Table (deviceName, siteId, health_score, ap_status), single value of degraded or down APs, time chart of health_score.

references.title: Catalyst Center Device Health API — Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**Wireless controller upgrade causing APs to temporarily unregister.** During a WLC firmware upgrade, associated APs lose their controller connection and may show as `Unreachable` or have degraded health scores for 10–30 minutes until they rejoin the upgraded controller. Distinguish by checking whether all affected APs share the same controller and whether the controller itself shows a recent reboot in `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth"`. Suppress by adding WLC upgrade windows to a `catalyst_maintenance_windows` lookup and excluding AP health alerts during those windows.

**AP firmware download and preimage staging.** When Catalyst Center pushes firmware to APs via SWIM, APs may show brief health-score dips during the download phase. The AP remains operational during download; the actual reload happens during the maintenance window. Distinguish by correlating with `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim"` for AP devices with `upgradeStatus=IN_PROGRESS`. Suppress by filtering APs with active firmware downloads from health alerting.

**Temporary RF conditions affecting AP health score.** AP health scores incorporate RF metrics. A temporary interference source (microwave oven, Bluetooth device, adjacent-channel AP) can lower the AP's health score without indicating an AP hardware problem. Distinguish by checking `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:wireless:rf"` for elevated `interferencePercentage` or `channelUtilization` on the affected AP. Suppress by requiring AP health to remain below threshold for 2+ consecutive polls before alerting.

**AP in disabled or maintenance mode intentionally.** APs may be administratively disabled for renovation, relocation, or decommissioning. They report as unreachable but this is expected. Distinguish by checking the AP's admin status in Catalyst Center > Assurance > Device Health or in `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:device"` for AP devices with `role` indicating disabled/maintenance. Suppress by maintaining a `catalyst_excluded_devices` lookup for APs in planned disabled state.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) ≥1.0 installed on search heads, indexers, and the heavy forwarder running the modular inputs.
• TA modular input **devicehealth** enabled, writing to `index=catalyst` with `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:devicehealth`.
• Catalyst Center **2.3.5+** so that `deviceType` values include both "Unified AP" and "ACCESS_POINT" (field naming varies across versions — validate with `| stats count by deviceType | search deviceType=*AP*`).
• RBAC: service account with **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE** or **SUPER-ADMIN-ROLE** to read Assurance device health data.
• License: each devicehealth poll generates ~1 event per managed device; AP-only filtering reduces the working set but does not reduce ingest.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
The TA polls `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/device-health` at the configured interval (default 900 seconds / 15 minutes). Each event represents one managed device. APs appear as entries with `deviceType` containing "AP" or "ACCESS_POINT" depending on Catalyst Center version and device model.

Key fields for AP health monitoring:
• `overallHealth` (integer 0-100): composite health score from Assurance analytics, incorporating CPU, memory, link, and reachability factors.
• `reachabilityHealth`: "Reachable" or "Unreachable" — based on ICMP/SNMP reachability from Catalyst Center.
• `deviceName`: AP hostname (e.g., "AP-Floor3-IDF-A").
• `deviceType`: platform type — filter on `*AP*` to isolate access points from switches and routers.
• `siteId`: UUID of the site hierarchy location.
• `platformId`: hardware PID (e.g., "AIR-AP2802I-B-K9", "C9120AXI-B").

Expected volume: in a 2,000-AP deployment at 900s poll interval, expect ~2,000 events per poll cycle = ~192,000 AP health events/day.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth" deviceType="Unified AP" OR deviceType="ACCESS_POINT"
| stats latest(overallHealth) as health_score latest(reachabilityHealth) as reachability count as poll_events by deviceName, deviceType, siteId, platformId
| eval health_status=case(reachability="Unreachable","Unreachable", health_score>=75,"Healthy", health_score>=50,"Fair", health_score>=25,"Poor", 1==1,"Critical")
| sort health_score
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`deviceType="Unified AP" OR deviceType="ACCESS_POINT"`**: Filters to APs only. Both values are checked because Catalyst Center uses different type strings across versions and AP models. Run `| stats count by deviceType | search deviceType=*AP*` on your data to confirm which values appear.
• **`stats latest(overallHealth) ... by deviceName`**: Collapses to the most recent poll per AP, preventing duplicate counting from multiple poll cycles in the time window.
• **`eval health_status=case(...)`**: Applies the same health banding as Catalyst Center Assurance (75/50/25 thresholds). An AP that is Unreachable gets its own status regardless of the last known health score.
• **`sort health_score`**: Surfaces the worst-scoring APs at the top for operational triage.

For alerting on unreachable APs: `| where reachability="Unreachable" | table deviceName, siteId, platformId` — schedule every 15 minutes, trigger on `count > 0`, throttle per `deviceName` for 30 minutes.

Step 3 — Validate
• **UI comparison:** Open Catalyst Center > Assurance > Health > Device Health, filter by "Access Points." Compare the health score and reachability status for 3-5 APs with the Splunk results within the same poll window. Differences of 1-2 points are normal due to poll timing.
• **Count check:** `| stats dc(deviceName) as ap_count | compare to Catalyst Center > Assurance > Health summary tile for "Total Access Points."` The counts should match within ±1% (minor differences from poll timing or newly discovered APs).
• **Field validation:** Verify `platformId` matches the expected AP model for each device (e.g., C9120 for Wi-Fi 6, C9130 for Wi-Fi 6E). Spot-check 2-3 APs in Catalyst Center Inventory to confirm.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard panel:** Single-value tile for "Unreachable APs" (red when >0), stacked bar chart for health distribution by status (Healthy/Fair/Poor/Critical/Unreachable), and a table of the bottom-10 APs by health score.
• **Alert:** Scheduled every 15 minutes; trigger when any AP transitions from Reachable to Unreachable. Throttle per deviceName for 30 minutes to avoid flapping alerts during reboots.
• **Runbook integration:** Unreachable AP → check PoE on the upstream switch port, verify AP LED status, check Catalyst Center > Assurance > Device 360 for the AP.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **All APs show Unreachable:** TA service account lost API access, or Catalyst Center Assurance is down. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ExecProcessor "TA_cisco_catalyst"` for authentication errors.
• **Health score disagrees with Catalyst Center UI:** Poll timing difference — the TA may have captured the score during a transient health dip that has since recovered. Compare `_time` of the Splunk event with the "Last Updated" timestamp in Device 360.
• **Missing APs:** Not all APs appear in the search — check whether the TA's `devicehealth` input pagination is completing. In `index=_internal`, look for pagination-related errors. Also verify that the APs are fully onboarded (not in Provisioning state) in Catalyst Center.
• **`deviceType` filter not matching:** Run `| stats count by deviceType` to identify the exact string used for APs in your Catalyst Center version. Update the filter accordingly.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the health of every wireless access point to make sure they are all working properly, catching problems before people lose their WiFi.
